marketState: neutral-to-bearish medium-term, short-term propped up by strong near-term weather demand
summary: NG=F front (Aug26) trades at $2.919, essentially at model fair value ($2.917) and comfortably inside the $2.417-$3.417 band, so there is no valuation extreme to lean on directly. Medium-term the tilt is bearish: price sits 10.9pp below its seasonal median, 4-week seasonality odds favor a decline (only 31% chance higher, typical move -3.1%), storage carries a +184 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average after a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, and managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts), though only at the 35th percentile so not stretched. Near-term the picture is more constructive: 1-7 day cooling demand is running +25.7% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, the curve shows slight backwardation into September, and the daily chart is oversold (RSI 35.6, price below the lower Bollinger band) - all consistent with near-term tightness and a possible bounce even as the daily/weekly trend stays bearish. Seasonality reliability is explicitly flagged Low (50pp 10-90% spread this week), which caps conviction on the medium-term call.
